---
title: "Capillary Hairsedge (Bulbostylis ciliatifolia)"
slug: "bulbostylis-ciliatifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622295240/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Capillary Hairsedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622295240/medium.jpg" alt="Capillary Hairsedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/dsandii">
dsandii</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341794358">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=159594)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to dry sandy habitats, savannas, and disturbed areas.

## Natural history notes

A fine-textured, grass-like plant characterized by its slender stems, narrow leaves, and small clustered spikelets. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub, sandhills, and open flatwoods. It grows during the warmer months and often occurs as a subtle component of groundcover vegetation in these ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/126699-Buchnera-americana)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3219)
